posts.title: Improving Productivity: The 5-Minute Block Scheduling Method
posts.summary: Ditch the traditional to-do list for calendar-focused block schedules to regain focus, prevent burnout, and manage cognitive energy levels.
posts.content:
Most to-do lists fail because they tell you *what* to do, but not *when* to do it. Block scheduling maps tasks directly to short blocks of time in your day.

### Why Lists Fail
To-do lists create choice fatigue. When you finish a task, looking at a list of 10 more tasks forces your brain to decide what to do next, depleting willpower.

### Implementing Block Schedules
1. **Batch Similar Work:** Put email replies, content reading, and administrative tasks into dedicated 30-minute slots.
2. **Deep Work Blocks:** Schedule 90-minute blocks for deep cognitive tasks like programming or planning, turning off all push alerts.
3. **Energy Management:** Put high-priority tasks in your morning peak focus hours and low-priority tasks in your post-lunch slump.

Give it a try this week and witness how much cognitive space you recover!
posts.author: user_initial_1

comments.author: user_initial_2
comments.content: This is a great write-up! I've been experimenting with React 19 in beta and the action hook cleanup is genuinely refreshing compared to traditional useEffect form tracking.

comments.content: The border detail is extremely underrated! Adding that 1px solid translucent white line transforms flat elements into looking like real polished glass.
